content.text:
Yes. That edge case is real, but it is narrower than the mobile bug we just fixed.

What the ignored test is exercising:
- It uses `CodexGateway` directly, not the full bridge state machine.
- It creates a fresh Codex thread, then immediately starts the first turn on that same fresh thread.
- The relevant paths are [gateway.rs:229](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:229) and [gateway.rs:1113](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:1113).

Why it fails:
1. `create_thread(...)` calls `thread/start`, gets back a thread id, and then tries to `thread/read` it immediately.
2. If that immediate read says “thread not found”, the code treats that as a transient case, returns an optimistic snapshot from the `thread/start` payload, and preserves the transport for reuse. That is here: [gateway.rs:254](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:254) through [gateway.rs:262](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:262).
3. Then `start_turn_streaming(...)` takes that reserved transport and immediately does an unconditional `resume_thread(...)` before `turn/start`. That is here: [gateway.rs:345](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:345) through [gateway.rs:359](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:359).
4. The fallback logic in `start_turn_with_resume(...)` only knows one heuristic: if the error contains `"thread not found"`, call `thread/resume` and try `turn/start` again. That heuristic is defined by [gateway.rs:1054](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:1054) and used in [gateway.rs:1121](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:1121).

The problem with that heuristic:
- For a genuinely existing older thread, `"thread not found"` can mean “this connection has not loaded that thread yet”, so `thread/resume` is reasonable.
- For a brand-new thread, `"thread not found"` can instead mean “the app-server has acknowledged thread creation, but the thread is not yet materialized/readable/resumable”.
- In that case `thread/resume` is the wrong fallback, because there may be no rollout yet, which is why we saw:
  - `thread/resume failed: no rollout found`
  - then `turn/start failed: thread not found`

So the real issue is:
- `should_resume_thread(...)` is too coarse.
- It treats all `"thread not found"` cases as “resume this thread”, but fresh-thread startup is a different state from reconnecting to an existing thread.

Why this is separate from the Samsung/mobile fix:
- The Samsung bug was the bridge transport never sending `initialized`, so live notifications were gated off entirely.
- Fixing that restored live streaming end-to-end.
- The ignored gateway test is a direct-gateway race on the first turn of a just-created thread. It can still fail even though the full bridge-backed mobile flow now works.

What I think the proper fix would be:
- Do not blindly `thread/resume` on a just-created reserved transport.
- Distinguish “fresh thread still materializing” from “existing thread needs resume”.
- For the fresh-thread case, retry `turn/start` with a short backoff, or wait until `thread/read` succeeds on that same transport, instead of treating it as a resume problem.

In short:
- Mobile streaming bug: fixed.
- Direct gateway ignored test: still exposing a startup race in the fresh-thread path.
- Root cause there: the gateway conflates “new thread not ready yet” with “old thread not resumed yet”.

2. If that immediate read says “thread not found”, the code treats that as a transient case, returns an optimistic snapshot from the `thread/start` payload, and preserves the transport for reuse. That is here: [gateway.rs:254](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:254) through [gateway.rs:262](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:262).

That heuristic is defined by [gateway.rs:1054](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:1054) and used in [gateway.rs:1121](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/crates/bridge-core/src/server/gateway.rs:1121).

  - `thread/resume failed: no rollout found`

content.text:
Not fully, but partly yes.

What happens on turn finish:
- When a live `thread_status_changed` arrives with a non-`running` status, the controller intentionally schedules a snapshot refresh in [_handleLiveEvent(...)](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart:1288).
- That path calls [_refreshThreadSnapshotFromBridge()](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart:1491), which fetches:
  - thread detail again
  - the first timeline page again
- Then it does `state = state.copyWith(thread: ..., items: items, ...)`, so the timeline list gets a new `items` list object.

Important nuance:
- It is not throwing away the timeline and rebuilding from zero.
- It merges the refreshed page into current live items through [_mergeTimelineEntries(...)](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart:927), so it tries to preserve/merge existing live events rather than replace everything blindly.

But:
- it still reassigns the full `items` list
- it recomputes timeline blocks in the UI
- and now that the running card is part of the timeline again, that last “in progress” item disappears exactly when the completion refresh lands

So a visible flash at completion is plausible, yes.

Short version:
- “Reload everything?” No, not all history and not from scratch.
- “Do we refetch and replace the visible timeline model on completion?” Yes.

If you want, I can smooth this next by making completion avoid the full timeline snapshot refresh unless we actually need missing archive-only items, or by applying a less disruptive merge so the list does not visually flash when the turn settles.

content.text:
Not as a blanket rule, no.

For Codex, a completion refresh is useful for two reasons:
- the final archive/snapshot can contain authoritative end-state data that live deltas may not fully capture
- it helps recover if the live stream dropped something near the end

So the idea itself is sound. The problem is doing a full visible-timeline catch-up every time a turn leaves `running`, even when live streaming was healthy. That is probably heavier than necessary now.

My view:
- Keep a lightweight detail/status refresh on completion: yes.
- Keep a full timeline snapshot refresh on every successful Codex completion: probably no.
- Do the heavier timeline refresh only when there is evidence we need it.

Good triggers for a full timeline refresh:
- live stream was disconnected or errored
- we completed without seeing meaningful assistant/tool live activity
- there is pending approval/user-input state that needs authoritative cleanup
- terminal status arrived but the last live item still looks incomplete

If live streaming was healthy, the better behavior is:
- trust the live timeline you already have
- refresh only thread metadata/final status
- skip replacing the visible timeline list

So: the current behavior was defensible while the stream path was unreliable, but after fixing Codex streaming it is probably no longer the right default. If you want, I can change it to conditional refresh so Codex completion stops flashing the list.

Best plan is:

1. Make Codex completion refresh conditional in [thread_detail_controller.dart](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-m...

content.text:
Best plan is:

1. Make Codex completion refresh conditional in [thread_detail_controller.dart](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart).
   - Track per-turn live health:
     - saw meaningful assistant/tool live activity
     - saw stream error/disconnect
     - saw pending approval/input weirdness
   - On terminal `thread_status_changed`:
     - always do `_refreshThreadDetailFromBridge()` for final metadata/status
     - only do `_refreshThreadSnapshotFromBridge()` if the turn looked unhealthy

2. Define “unhealthy” narrowly.
   - Do full snapshot refresh only when:
     - live stream disconnected or errored during the turn
     - completion arrived without meaningful live assistant/tool activity
     - pending input/approval state looks stale
     - turn failed/interrupted
   - If live streaming was healthy, keep the current `items` list and do not rebuild the visible timeline.

3. Preserve the current merge behavior as fallback.
   - We keep the full snapshot merge path for safety.
   - We just stop using it on every normal successful Codex completion.

4. Add one small deterministic guard test.
   - A controller-level test for the “should snapshot-refresh on completion” decision.
   - That gives stability without relying only on live device runs.

5. Re-run the Samsung live E2E after the change.
   - Use the same [live_codex_thread_status_order_test.dart](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/integration_test/live_codex_thread_status_order_test.dart).
   - I would extend it slightly to fail if, after first live assistant activity appears, the rendered timeline temporarily collapses/resets before final completion.

Why this is the best balance:
- Stability: we still have the authoritative snapshot fallback when Codex live delivery is suspicious.
- No flash: healthy Codex turns stay on the already-streamed timeline and only metadata gets refreshed.
- Low risk: we are not removing the recovery path, only gating it.

If you want, I can implement exactly that now and rerun the Samsung E2E.
